items.q: What are the four Ps of the marketing mix?
items.correct: Product, Price, Place (distribution), and Promotion — the controllable variables a business uses to satisfy customers and achieve objectives.
items.misconception: The four Ps are Product, Price, Profit, and People.
items.random_wrong: The four Ps are Planning, Producing, Promoting, and Profit.
items.why: Students often substitute 'profit' or 'people' into the four Ps. The classic McCarthy framework is Product, Price, Place and Promotion — extended models add others but those four are foundational.

items.q: What is a brand in marketing?
items.correct: A combination of name, design, symbols and associations that distinguishes a product and creates customer perception and loyalty — more than just a logo.
items.misconception: A brand is just the logo on a product.
items.random_wrong: A brand is the company that makes a product.
items.why: Branding is the sum of perceptions, not just visual identity. Coca-Cola the brand is much more than the script logo — it is associations, taste expectations, advertising history.

items.q: What is corporate governance, and how does it differ from CSR?
items.correct: Corporate governance is the system of rules, practices and processes by which a company is directed and controlled — focusing on accountability, board structure and shareholder rights. CSR is about wider social/environmental responsibility.
items.misconception: Corporate governance is the same as corporate social responsibility.
items.random_wrong: Corporate governance is the legal requirement to have a CEO.
items.why: Governance is about how the company is run (board, audit, transparency); CSR is about what the company does for society. Students conflate them — both concern ethics but at different levels.

items.q: Choosing whether to sell online, in shops, or both, is mainly a decision about which P?
items.why: It's Place. “Product” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which is part of the PRODUCT element of the mix?
items.why: It's Packaging design. “TV advert” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Cost per unit £20. Mark-up 50%. What is the selling price, in £?
items.why: Exact answer: 30. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
